description: Trader mode is a new feature for Web3 wallet and DEX that transforms a regular EOA account into a TEE (Trusted Execution Environment)-based account. Trader mode gives users access to more advanced trading modes and features.
An OTC version of our Structured Products - only available to VIPs through their VIP relationship managers.

Deep dives is a section in our app that aims to provide info that goes beyond the 'how', to explain the 'why'. 
It signals depth over headlines, and sets an expectation of expert insight, context, and substance.

An alert is a system-generated notification that draws the user’s attention to important information, status changes, risks, or required actions. Alerts are typically time-sensitive and appear in-app, as a banner, modal, push notification, or message.
Links that lead to scam or harmful sites (phishing, malware, fake logins).

The currency used as collateral to open and maintain a margin position. When trading on margin, the margin currency is held in your account as a security deposit against potential losses. The required margin amount is calculated based on your position size and the applicable margin rate.

A promotional reward for first-time users after completing specific steps (e.g., sign-up, KYC, first deposit/trade).
Usually has conditions like minimum amounts, time limits, and withdrawal/trading restrictions. 中文有时候也用“新手福利/新手礼包”
Lending: Providing crypto to a borrower or lending market in exchange for interest/yield, with repayment due later (often collateral-backed).
The amount of crypto a user can lend out to other borrowers.
Quantitative measures used to track and evaluate performance, activity, or risk (e.g., volume, APR, TVL, PnL).
Most often used when referring to users’ trading fee tiers or VIP levels.

Token Generation Events (TGEs) are a significant milestone in the lifecycle of a blockchain project. These events mark the official launch of the project's token and are often held concurrently with airdrops to maximize visibility and user engagement.
The percentage that shows how much profit an asset netted.

AVL is a line that represents the average price level over a selected period. It smooths short-term fluctuations and helps assess whether the current price is relatively high or low, serving as a reference for trend direction and potential support or resistance.
A red packet shower is part of our Chinese new year campaign where users can claim red packets that contain crypto rewards for a limited time.
A machine-readable code consisting of an array of black and white squares, typically used for storing URLs or other information for reading by the camera on a smartphone.

AVL is a line that represents the average price level over a selected period. It smooths short-term fluctuations and helps assess whether the current price is relatively high or low, serving as a reference for trend direction and potential support or resistance.

The MA helps to level the price data over a specified period by creating a constantly updated average price.
A simple moving average (SMA) is a calculation that takes the arithmetic mean of a given set of prices over a specific number of days in the past.

The MA helps to level the price data over a specified period by creating a constantly updated average price.
A simple moving average (SMA) is a calculation that takes the arithmetic mean of a given set of prices over a specific number of days in the past.

Weight Moving Average (WMA) is a moving average that assigns more weight to recent prices and less to older ones. Compared to a Simple Moving Average (MA), WMA responds faster to price changes, making it especially useful for identifying short-term trend shifts.

Weight Moving Average (WMA) is a moving average that assigns more weight to recent prices and less to older ones. Compared to a Simple Moving Average (MA), WMA responds faster to price changes, making it especially useful for identifying short-term trend shifts.
MS stands for Short Message Service. It is a text messaging service that allows the exchange of short text messages between mobile devices.
The task center is where users visit to complete tasks in order to remove risk control restrictions on their account.
When used in Rule Engine project in Risk squad, "actions" should be understood as risk control penalties.
A lock-up period is a period of time where specific tokens cannot be sold, transferred, or traded.

1. In scenarios that highlight its social features, the term can be expanded as "Orbit community" or "星球社区".
2. OKX Orbit should be translated into "欧易星球".
Momentum catcher is a trading product that lets you earn from price swings by buying option contracts based on your market outlook.
Large Language Models (LLMs) are AI systems that understand context and process data to generate intelligent, structured outputs. More used in Trading
Rebase the action means adjusting a pre-market perpetual contract's face value, mark price, and user's average opening price.
An APR voucher is a type of voucher that once applied, will slightly boost the user's APR for a limited time.

The function as a whole. Under this function, users can see their 1-1 chats, group chats, notifications, alerts etc.
“消息”作为整体功能名。“消息“”功能可以让用户查看一对一聊天、群聊、通知、提醒等内容。
Margin Rewards is the renamed Simple Earn (flexible term) for EEA markets.

Pre-market rebase perpetuals is a new type of pre-market perpetuals. If the total supply of the underlying crypto (base currency) is significantly different from the pre-determined amount, the trading of this contract will be suspended to allow for a rebase. Rebasing meaning, based on the contract's actual total supply, we will adjust the contract's face value, mark price, and the average opening price of user's positions.

The tradeback voucher is a new reward type that allows users to receive cashback on eligible trading activities.
It aims to support marketing campaigns, user acquisition, and retention by giving users tangible benefits tied to their trading activity. 
Currently supports spot and futures trading.

Its meaning is context-dependent. It can refer to the active participation and interaction of users within a crypto ecosystem — such as trading, staking, voting, or participating in community and on-chain activities. In crypto trading, exposure can also mean the amount of risk a trader is under. It's the size of your position that is subject to price risk.
In the context of marketing campaigns and competitions, for example "P2P trading challenge".
The OKX Security Assistant is a built-in security feature designed to help protect users accounts and respond to potential threats.

Orbiter is Orbit's entry-level user category. Anyone who joins Orbit becomes an Orbiter.
The full Chinese naming for Orbiter is 星球居民, with 居民 as the short form. Chinese content designers have the flexibility to use either the full or short form depending on context. In English contexts, using "Orbiter" alone is sufficient.

Orbiter+ is the premium tier for Orbit users, upgraded from Orbiter with additional entry requirements such as being VIP.
The full Chinese naming for Orbiter+ is 星球领航员, with 领航员 as the short form. Chinese content designers have the flexibility to use either the full or short form depending on context. In English contexts, using "Orbiter+" alone is sufficient.
For a fee, P2P merchants can set one of their ads as a paid ad and this ad will display as a top ad in the P2P marketplace.
Auto-confirm is a feature on OKX that allows users to place orders instantly without a password or face scan.
Auto-stake on OKX is a feature that automatically stakes your eligible crypto assets so they can start earning rewards without requiring manual action each time. Can also be used as a verb.
Real-world assets (RWAs) are physical or traditional financial assets that exist outside the blockchain.
OKX VIP is both our VIP program and how we address a single VIP user.

Auto-earn is a VIP exclusive feature for now. Automatically lend or stake the funds in your trading account without affecting your trades. You can continue to use those funds for open orders and margin.
VIP users can gift others a VIP trial. Trial users can experience discounted fee rates and priority service.
A withdrawal of funds from one OKX account to another OKX account without the funds leaving the platform

Community refers to a group of users, participants, or stakeholders who are connected by a shared interest, platform, product, or goal and who interact, contribute, and exchange value with one another.
Content creation is the process of planning, producing, and publishing written, visual, audio, or interactive materials to inform, educate, engage, or convert an audience.

X Drops Club is a program for exclusive airdrops automatically deposited into user account and can be used towards trades or withdrawn. X Drops Club is your shortcut to a safe and easy way to earn airdrops on repeat.
https://www.okx.com/x-drops?pagesource=header
The filled price is the actual price at which an order (or part of an order) is executed in the market.
An accredited investor is someone who meets certain requirements which grant them access to a wider range of products in Singapore, in line with local regulations.

Auto-renewal is a feature in Smart Account that allows the enable period of the smart account to be extended automatically every 7, 15, or 30 days. It can also appear in verb or adj. forms to adapt to different contexts:
e.g. Auto-renew your smart account's enable period.
e.g. Your smart account is auto-renewed.
OKX rewards users with bonus funds equivalent to a percentage of their net new deposits
xBETH is a wrapped version of BETH on X Layer
X Campaign is a Web3 product under Boost. The Trading Competition and Giveaway pages will be merged into X Campaign.
X Launch is a Web3 product under Boost. Users who have traded on OKX DEX can participate in X Launch events to earn rewards.
A service fee is a flat fee that OKX DEX charges for using the OKX DEX interface to access and conduct transactions on third-party DEXs.

Use “network fee” when you’re referring to an onchain transaction that uses the computation power of a blockchain. As a rule of thumb, we need to let users know that this is charged irrespective of the success of the transaction. “Gas fee" is reserved for EVM-based chains only. Gas fee 为 ETH 专用，中文统一为“网络费用”

Use “network fee” when you’re referring to an onchain transaction that uses the computation power of a blockchain. As a rule of thumb, we need to let users know that this is charged irrespective of the success of the transaction. “Gas fee" is reserved for EVM-based chains only. Gas fee 为 ETH 专用，中文统一为“网络费用”
Face value is a financial term used to describe the nominal or dollar value of a security, as stated by its issuer.

Factory contract is a smart contract that programmatically deploys other contracts from a single template. It creates multiple identical or parameterized instances on demand, ensuring consistent logic and reduced deployment overhead. In our context, the factory creates the airdrop reward contracts for OKX Boost.
Token/project name.

An insurance sector that covers against risks associated with DeFi investments, smart contract failures, wallet issues, and dApp/DAO hacks.

In another meaning (”blockchain insurance”) can be understood as traditional insurance that utilizes blockchain technology for more efficient and effective insurance services.

Onchain Indicator: Refers to metrics or data points that are captured and stored on a blockchain network. These indicators provide information about various aspects of the network, such as the amount of tokens in circulation, the volume of transactions, and other key metrics. They can be used to gain insight into the health and activity of a blockchain network and to inform investment decisions.
Cryptocurrency transactions that occur on the blockchain and remain dependent on the state of the blockchain for their validity
Onchain Earn is a collection of Earn products that used to be called DeFI and ETH2.0.
Describes technology or processes that enable assets or data to move between different blockchain networks.

A sequencer is a critical component within Layer 2 networks. Its main function is to receive and execute transactions from Layer 2 users. It then orders and compresses these transactions into a batch, which is finally submitted to Layer 1.
A certain percentage of copy traders' profits will be paid to the lead trader to reward their services. This process is called profit-sharing.
Flash Earn is an innovative earn product that lets you subscribe with USDT or other major crypto. Earn base crypto yields and get bonus airdrops of newly listed assets.
A beginner-friendly trading feature on OKX that lets users quickly purchase or sell crypto using fiat currencies or payment methods such as bank transfer, card, or third-party providers.

A feature that lets you sell crypto instantly at a quoted price. The price is locked for a short time while you confirm, protecting you from sudden market changes. The quote includes a small spread, so you receive slightly less than the current market rate, but no extra fees are added after you confirm. You can use fiat to buy crypto and vice versa

A feature that lets you buy crypto instantly at a quoted price. The price is locked for a short time while you confirm, protecting you from sudden market changes. The quote includes a small spread, so you pay slightly more than the current market rate, but no extra fees are added afterward. You can use fiat to buy crypto and vice versa
A list of best lead traders available to copy on the copy trading page
Product slogan for OKX Pay

The security fund, previously known as the insurance fund, is used to offset potential losses from forced liquidations. When a trader's position is liquidated, any excess assets are added to the security fund. If the security fund is depleted, then the exchange must trigger ADL.
Legal name of a bank account holder
The Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCAR) is a European Union regulatory framework designed to standardize crypto-asset rules across all EU member states.
An action that adds funds or assets to a user’s OKX account or wallet.
An official message or announcement.
The distribution of assets, rewards, or tokens to users based on specific criteria such as staking amount, subscription size, or participation level.
A real-time bank transfer method within the Single Euro Payments Area (SEPA) that allows users to deposit or withdraw euros instantly — 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.
A feature that automatically sells or converts assets when certain conditions are met — for example, after receiving staking rewards, completing a subscription, or earning yield.
A record of user account data or asset balances at a specific point in time.
Information that identifies where a user’s deposited money or crypto assets originate from.
A setting that automatically enrolls a user’s eligible assets into an Earn or savings product once they become available.
A beginner-friendly trading feature on OKX that lets users quickly purchase or sell crypto using fiat currencies or payment methods such as bank transfer, card, or third-party providers.

An instruction submitted by the user to withdraw or unlock assets from a staking, lending, or savings product.
Once a redemption request is made, the assets enter the redemption period before they become fully available for use or withdrawal.

The time required to withdraw or unlock assets after ending a staking, lending, or savings product.
 During the redemption period, the assets are being processed for release and cannot be used or traded until they are fully available again.
An asset or token that has never been listed on OKX.
Act of withdrawing user's rewards (profit) from Earn products

Meme Pump is a discovery + trading tool aimed at aggregating and surfacing trending meme coins across multiple blockchains. It shows meme coins that are trending, regardless of whether they’ve migrated (i.e. moved from initial launch platforms to other exchanges or chains).
The overall value of funds a user has lent out on the platform, including both active and completed loans.
The total funds a user currently has lent out and earning interest.
OKX Earn in CEX is a product that provides users with a way to generate interest on their assets through multiple investment choices. Products include Simple Earn, Loan, and On-chain Earn.
A feature that lets users instantly swap one cryptocurrency for another at a quoted rate.

Owner (Crypto)
The individual or entity that holds and controls a cryptocurrency wallet’s private keys, thereby having full authority over the digital assets stored in that wallet.

A transfer is the movement of digital assets (e.g., Bitcoin, Ethereum, tokens) from one wallet address to another. Transfers are recorded on the blockchain, typically requiring confirmation by the network.
The right or ability to enter, view, use, or modify data, systems, applications, or resources.

Wallet release is previous named "emergency escape". This feature allows users who are no longer eligible for, or decide to offboard from our services, to release their onchain assets from the platform to another EOA wallet. Usually it comes in the form of a noun, but it can be made into a verb phrase: e.g. release your wallet.

An OKX account mode designed for advanced traders. In futures mode, users can trade spot, margin and derivatives. Traders can then choose multi-currency or portfolio mode depending on whether they want higher capital efficiency or easier hedging against risks.

An advanced OKX account mode designed for professional traders. In multi-currency margin mode, users can trade spot, margin and derivatives. Crypto assets are valued in USD when calculating margin. Cross positions with same or different margin crypto share the risk, which can reduce the overall liquidation risk.
Name of a Web3 product. Boost allows users to join different events to earn token rewards.

A basic OKX account mode designed for spot and futures traders. In futures mode, users can trade spot, margin and derivatives. Trading symbols with the same margin crypto share the margin, and cross positions using the same margin crypto share the risk.
The total equity across multiple accounts
Direct swap between cryptocurrencies without traditional trading.
Perpetual contracts that let traders speculate on an asset’s price before the spot market opens, offering early price discovery but with higher risk.
Highly developed or more complex than basic level, mostly refers to tools.
OKX Planet is a social media feature, which combines the OKX Feed with an IM feature where users can follow each other, post content, and have discussions with each other.

OKX Builder is essentially a new system of tasks. Only use "OKX" in this section to maintain consistency with the promotion. 
The core positioning of KOC (OKX Builder) product is to link and improve the product ecology of Referral and Affiliate product matrix, and to tap and cultivate high-quality Referral and platform traders with potential through productization, so that they can become high-quality Affiliates of the platform.
Dust means low-value non-native tokens. Dust converter is a feature that allows an one-time swap of multiple such tokens (dust) to native tokens.
FRO is an acronym for Fraud Risk Operations.

Pre-market perpetuals is the product name of OKX's pre-market trading platform. It allows you to trade perpetual contracts on crypto that has not yet been officially listed on OKX. These futures, known as pre-market perpetual futures, track the price of these pre-listed crypto. They get converted to standard perpetual contracts once the crypto gets listed on OKX for spot trading. If the crypto doesn't get listed, pre-market perpetuals trading will end and the contracts will settle.
A unique invite link is a special, one-of-a-kind URL that gives the user  access to a group, a feature etc.
This role focuses on identifying and generating new business opportunities rather than closing deals.
An emergency escape usually refers to a mechanism or action that allows users to quickly withdraw, secure, or move their crypto assets in the event of a security threat or system failure.

A sticker is a digital image or illustration that users can send in chats or posts to express emotions, reactions, or messages. Unlike emojis, stickers are often larger, more detailed, and can be animated.

A Crypto Asset Service Provider (CASP) is a legal or natural entity that provides services related to crypto assets, which include cryptocurrencies, tokens, and other digital assets. The term is widely used in regulatory contexts, especially in jurisdictions like the European Union under the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation.
Referring to an asset no longer being denominated.
Referring to denomination: An asset being listed.
A discount rate is the rate at which you discount future cash flows or utility value back to its present value.
AUM stands for “Assets Under Management”.
Expiries mark the deadline by which the holder must exercise their rights.
A user that takes out a loan from another user under an agreement to pay it back later, typically with interest.
A lender is a user that extends credit to other entities, with the expectation that the full amount of the loan will be repaid.
A threshold price set to warn traders before they place an order that might be risky.
Price level is the average of current prices of cryptocurrencies and assets.
A "scale order" is a trading strategy where a large order is broken down into smaller, sequential orders placed at different price levels.
A validator is a participant in a blockchain network responsible for verifying transactions and maintaining the integrity and security of the blockchain.

Terms of use, also known as terms and conditions, are a set of rules and guidelines that govern how a website, app, or service can be used. They outline what users can and cannot do, and what rights the service provider maintains, essentially acting as a contract between the provider and the user
The action of lending an asset.
Terms of Service (ToS), also known as Terms of Use or Terms and Conditions, are a legal agreement between OKX and the users of our service.
This is just for the Earn subscription context. ​If we for example say "subscribe to our newsletter" we would use "Abonniere unseren Newsletter".
Term for a campaign to deposit and start trading ATH to earn rewards on OKX.
Ultimate Beneficial Owners (UBOs) refer to those who own 10% or more of a company’s shares. This term is usually used in the Compliance squad for the KYB verification process.

As we now offer crypto derivatives in Brazil, we’re launching a new type of account that offers access to derivatives via an offshore entity. Following Brazilian colleagues’ suggestion, we now name it as “Global Trading account” with “Global Trading” non-translatable and capitalized for 2 reasons:
1. “Global” will be recognized by Brazilians and easily understood, while “Trading” clearly differentiates it from anything associated with Pay or Card and keeps it tailored to our target user.
2. Keeping the name always in English also helps us strengthen our requirement that this be clearly a separate experience and product from OKX Brazil.
A savings feature designed for OKX TR users to earn competitive overnight interest on their Turkish Lira balances without manual transfers.
Chaintelligence is an on-chain data analysis tool from OKLink. It is a compound word by integrating Chain and Intelligence and used as the product name.
It's the address one party uses to receive onchain assets.
AA account is the smart-contract wallet account, an account that uses Account Abstraction (AA).

The umbrella phrase we use to describe any laws and regulations that might affect the KYC process. Instead of naming specific laws, we should only use "local laws" for simplicity. This phrase replaced "Travel Rule".
Used only for Travel Rule; equivalent to self-custody wallets.

"Aped" means that someone — like a person you follow, a smart investor, or an influencer (in the case of scan mode) — acted on the same impulse and jumped into the same investment/portfolio as you. The outcome can go either way, but the key idea is that you weren’t alone in taking the leap.

Aped in, Aped together, Aped with me => these terms are variations for the CN phrase "同车", depending on the context.
The overall profit or loss percentage across all positions or trading activities, providing a comprehensive performance measure.
The combined fees and charges for both opening and closing positions, representing total transaction costs.
Trading orders specifically designed to close existing positions, such as market or limit orders for exits.
The quantity or volume of assets being closed in a single order when exiting a position.
Orders or requests submitted to close existing positions, which may be pending execution.
The last traded price of an asset at the end of a trading session or when a position is terminated.
The final execution price at which a position is closed on the exchange.
The total fees and charges incurred when closing a trading position, including transaction fees and spreads.
The profit or loss percentage calculated when a position is closed, showing the final performance of the trade.
The bid-ask spread percentage at the time a position is opened, expressed as a rate.
The difference between the bid and ask prices when closing a position, affecting the final trade execution.
The difference between the bid and ask prices when opening a position, representing the immediate cost of entering a trade.
The bid-ask spread percentage at the time a position is closed, affecting the final execution price.
The specific criteria or requirements that must be met to open a new trading position, including minimum balance or risk parameters.
The specific criteria or requirements that must be met to close a trading position, such as price levels or time conditions.
The interest rate charged when borrowing assets for margin trading or short selling positions on OKX.

1. Previously called OKX DeFi or OKX Earn, (OKX) DeFi Earn is an all-in-one platform that offers various investment opportunities across different Web3 protocols on OKX Wallet.

2. Earn also refers to a suite of products on OKX and Okcoin that allow users to temporarily lend their crypto in exchange for crypto rewards.

1. Previously called OKX DeFi or OKX Earn, (OKX) DeFi Earn is an all-in-one platform that offers various investment opportunities across different Web3 protocols on OKX Wallet.

2. Earn also refers to a suite of products on OKX and Okcoin that allow users to temporarily lend their crypto in exchange for crypto rewards.
Feature name when connecting OKX Wallet with an exchange account.
Law enforcement refers to actions taken to ensure compliance with legal requirements. At OKX, this means adhering to the laws and regulations of each jurisdiction where we operate.
BTC Yield+ is a conservative strategy designed to generate an estimated 3% annual return in Bitcoin, regardless of market ups and downs. Subscriptions and redemptions are made directly in BTC.

A suite of OKX financial products designed to help users generate returns on their cryptocurrency holdings, including Earn products for staking and lending assets to earn interest or rewards, and Loan services for borrowing and lending to access liquidity or earn returns on deposits.

A "rate-up coupon" is a promotional tool, often used in the context of a trading bot campaign (usually the smart arbitrage bot), that offers a boost to the interest rate or yield on an investment. Essentially, it provides an additional, temporary, or exclusive benefit to a trader beyond the standard APR.
To launch/kick off an action. For OKEx and crypto trading, may refer to both "to trigger order" and "to trigger price".
to exchange one's onchain asset with a liquidity pool for another asset on the same blockchain.
to send free coins or tokens to wallet addresses to promote awareness of a crypto project within a crypto airdrop event.
OKX Web3 Security is an umbrella term for a set of pages and features under the OKX Web3 Security team, including malicious app/DApp/address detection, phising site identification etc.
As a user/trader, to "move position" means to move your current positions among your sub-accounts by using the "Move position" feature that is supported through API trading.
To audit means to perform a security review of a token’s properties to check if there are any contract exploits, hidden functions, or trading risks.

To perform an analysis and verification of a company's financial statements and records. Important Note: Please do not use "audit" together when referring to Proof of Reserves (PoR) we use alternatives such as: report, verification method, data capture. This is mandated by the GTM team.
Hedge: To use a Hedge strategy aiming to reducing the risk of investment by counterbalancing the potential losses of one investment with the gains of another.
The act of getting ownership of assets obtained through the OKX or Okcoin platform. For example earnings, rewards, or other promotions would all be claimed by someone.
to close a current position at market price and open a position of the same amount in the reserve direction.
to raise a dispute to try to solve the issue in such a situation: when a P2P order cannot proceed, one of the parties has the right.
to refuel using the "Refuel" Feature in DEX by using main network tokens as network fee.

A liquidation clearance fee is a fee imposed when a trader’s position is forcibly closed, or liquidated. This usually happens when the market moves against the position significantly in a very short term and the trader’s account lacks enough margin to maintain the position.
Avoid using liquidation fee, since the term “fee” may suggest a minor charge and imply that OKX profits from this situation, which is not the case. The goal is always to encourage prudent trading and risk management.
Campaign term. A voucher that is rewarded to campaign participants. Using this voucher allows the user to receive a random reward from the prize pool
A section in the OKX Feed platform that shows community posts with negative factors impacting an asset. To access open the app Discover → Opportunities → Feed → Top Mentions

In general, Airdrop Vouchers are type promotional vouchers that can be distributed to users by crypto projects or exchanges either for free or in exchange for a small promotional service, and they can be used/redeemed against a couple of different services related to that project or exchange that distributed them.
Here on OKX, it's used to refer to a type of voucher that allows a user to create a bot with a certain investment amount for free. If the bot makes a profit, it goes to the user. If the bot closes with a loss, it is covered by OKX.

Health factor is a numerical indicator that reflects the safety of your deposited assets relative to your borrowed assets and their underlying value. A higher health factor indicates a safer position and lower risk of liquidation. If the health factor less than or equal to 1, your assets may be subject to liquidation.

Pay is a service that streamlines crypto payments on our platform. For Chinese content, consistently use “Pay” across all products. In external communications like marketing, use the full brand name OKX Pay.
testing smartling glossary, ignore this term

Pre-open is a type of pre-listing phase, where traders can place limit orders before a crypto is officially listed in spot trading. It's called "Pre-open" because trading hasn't opened for the crypto. The listing price will be defined by the index price.

ZKP stands for a Zero-Knowledge Proof (ZKP) is a cryptographic technique that allows one party to prove to another that a statement is true, without revealing any information beyond the validity of the statement itself.

Centralized Exchange (CEX): A cryptocurrency exchange platform operated by a centralized entity that retains custody of crypto assets on a user's behalf. Centralized exchange refers to a digital platform that facilitates the trading, buying, and selling of various cryptocurrencies.
CEX stands for centralized exchange.

A Zero-Knowledge Proof (ZKP) is a cryptographic technique that allows one party to prove to another that a statement is true, without revealing any information beyond the validity of the statement itself.
Often used informally to describe a promising cryptocurrency or project that has significant potential for growth or profitability but may still be undervalued or not widely known.
It refers to the specific price at which costs and revenues are equal, resulting in neither profit nor loss.
It refers to the list or system that specifies the fees associated with different services or transactions

An incentive is something that motivates or encourages someone to take a specific action or behave in a certain way. It can be a reward, benefit, or encouragement designed to influence decisions or actions.

In general, KOLs are experts with a strong reputation in a specific field. 

In crypto industry, Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs) can play a crucial role in influencing and shaping public opinions around your crypto project, product, or service.

They have the expertise, credibility, and a huge follower base in their niche, which they can leverage to endorse your crypto products, services, or ideas, which makes them a brilliant source of organic promotion, marketing, and community building in the crypto space.
Impact value: The notional value used to determine the impact bid and ask price. Binance and Bybit call it "Impact margin notional". Ex.: Impact value ({ccy}), where {ccy} can be USDT, USDC, or USD.
BTC Select is a collection of BTC-USDT Dual Investment products designed to help users make use of their idle BTC.

Pre-quote is a type of pre-listing phase, where traders can place limit orders before a crypto is officially listed in spot trading. It's called "Pre-quote" because the final price of the asset hasn't been defined. The listing price will be defined by the index price.

Measures the average reward compared to the average risk of a position.

Risk/reward ratio = Avg PnL of losing positions / Avg PnL of winning positions

An OTC version of our Structured Products - only available to VIPs through their VIP relationship managers.

Links that lead to scam or harmful sites (phishing, malware, fake logins).

The currency used as collateral to open and maintain a margin position. When trading on margin, the margin currency is held in your account as a security deposit against potential losses. The required margin amount is calculated based on your position size and the applicable margin rate.

A promotional reward for first-time users after completing specific steps (e.g., sign-up, KYC, first deposit/trade).
Usually has conditions like minimum amounts, time limits, and withdrawal/trading restrictions. 中文有时候也用“新手福利/新手礼包”
Lending: Providing crypto to a borrower or lending market in exchange for interest/yield, with repayment due later (often collateral-backed).
The amount of crypto a user can lend out to other borrowers.
Quantitative measures used to track and evaluate performance, activity, or risk (e.g., volume, APR, TVL, PnL).

The overall profit or loss percentage across all positions or trading activities, providing a comprehensive performance measure.

The difference between the bid and ask prices when closing a position, affecting the final trade execution.
The difference between the bid and ask prices when opening a position, representing the immediate cost of entering a trade.
The bid-ask spread percentage at the time a position is closed, affecting the final execution price.
The specific criteria or requirements that must be met to open a new trading position, including minimum balance or risk parameters.
The specific criteria or requirements that must be met to close a trading position, such as price levels or time conditions.

A liquidation clearance fee is a fee imposed when a trader’s position is forcibly closed, or liquidated. This usually happens when the market moves against the position significantly in a very short term and the trader’s account lacks enough margin to maintain the position.
Avoid using liquidation fee, since the term “fee” may suggest a minor charge and imply that OKX profits from this situation, which is not the case. The goal is always to encourage prudent trading and risk management.

Factory contract is a smart contract that programmatically deploys other contracts from a single template. Onchain Indicator: Refers to metrics or data points that are captured and stored on a blockchain network. Pre-market perpetuals is the product name of OKX's pre-market trading platform. It allows you to trade perpetual contracts on crypto that has not yet been officially listed on OKX. These futures, known as pre-market perpetual futures, track the price of these pre-listed crypto. They get converted to standard perpetual contracts once the crypto gets listed on OKX for spot trading. If the crypto doesn't get listed, pre-market perpetuals trading will end and the contracts will settle.

A sticker is a digital image or illustration that users can send in chats or posts to express emotions, reactions, or messages. Unlike emojis, stickers are often larger, more detailed, and can be animated.

A Crypto Asset Service Provider (CASP) is a legal or natural entity that provides services related to crypto assets, which include cryptocurrencies, tokens, and other digital assets. Referring to an asset no longer being denominated.

As we now offer crypto derivatives in Brazil, we’re launching a new type of account that offers access to derivatives via an offshore entity. Following Brazilian colleagues’ suggestion, we now name it as “Global Trading account” with “Global Trading” non-translatable and capitalized for 2 reasons:
1. “Global” will be recognized by Brazilians and easily understood, while “Trading” clearly differentiates it from anything associated with Pay or Card and keeps it tailored to our target user.
2. Keeping the name always in English also helps us strengthen our requirement that this be clearly a separate experience and product from OKX Brazil.

"Aped" means that someone — like a person you follow, a smart investor, or an influencer (in the case of scan mode) — acted on the same impulse and jumped into the same investment/portfolio as you. The outcome can go either way, but the key idea is that you weren’t alone in taking the leap.

Aped in, Aped together, Aped with me => these terms are variations for the CN phrase "同车", depending on the context.
The overall profit or loss percentage across all positions or trading activities, providing a comprehensive performance measure.
The combined fees and charges for both opening and closing positions, representing total transaction costs.
Trading orders specifically designed to close existing positions, such as market or limit orders for exits.
The quantity or volume of assets being closed in a single order when exiting a position.
Orders or requests submitted to close existing positions, which may be pending execution.
The last traded price of an asset at the end of a trading session or when a position is terminated.
The final execution price at which a position is closed on the exchange.
The total fees and charges incurred when closing a trading position, including transaction fees and spreads.
The profit or loss percentage calculated when a position is closed, showing the final performance of the trade.
The bid-ask spread percentage at the time a position is opened, expressed as a rate.
The difference between the bid and ask prices when closing a position, affecting the final trade execution.
The difference between the bid and ask prices when opening a position, representing the immediate cost of entering a trade.
The bid-ask spread percentage at the time a position is closed, affecting the final execution price.
The specific criteria or requirements that must be met to open a new trading position, including minimum balance or risk parameters.
The specific criteria or requirements that must be met to close a trading position, such as price levels or time conditions.
The interest rate charged when borrowing assets for margin trading or short selling positions on OKX.

A suite of OKX financial products designed to help users generate returns on their cryptocurrency holdings, including Earn products for staking and lending assets to earn interest or rewards, and Loan services for borrowing and lending to access liquidity or earn returns on deposits.

Essentially, it provides an additional, temporary, or exclusive benefit to a trader beyond the standard APR.
To launch/kick off an action. For OKEx and crypto trading, may refer to both "to trigger order" and "to trigger price".
to exchange one's onchain asset with a liquidity pool for another asset on the same blockchain.
to send free coins or tokens to wallet addresses to promote awareness of a crypto project within a crypto airdrop event.

As a user/trader, to "move position" means to move your current positions among your sub-accounts by using the "Move position" feature that is supported through API trading.
To audit means to perform a security review of a token’s properties to check if there are any contract exploits, hidden functions, or trading risks.

To perform an analysis and verification of a company's financial statements and records. Important Note: Please do not use "audit" together when referring to Proof of Reserves (PoR) we use alternatives such as: report, verification method, data capture. This is mandated by the GTM team.
Hedge: To use a Hedge strategy aiming to reducing the risk of investment by counterbalancing the potential losses of one investment with the gains of another.
The act of getting ownership of assets obtained through the OKX or Okcoin platform. For example earnings, rewards, or other promotions would all be claimed by someone.
to close a current position at market price and open a position of the same amount in the reserve direction.
to raise a dispute to try to solve the issue in such a situation: when a P2P order cannot proceed, one of the parties has the right.
to refuel using the "Refuel" Feature in DEX by using main network tokens as network fee.

A liquidation clearance fee is a fee imposed when a trader’s position is forcibly closed, or liquidated. This usually happens when the market moves against the position significantly in a very short term and the trader’s account lacks enough margin to maintain the position.
Avoid using liquidation fee, since the term “fee” may suggest a minor charge and imply that OKX profits from this situation, which is not the case. The goal is always to encourage prudent trading and risk management.
Campaign term. A voucher that is rewarded to campaign participants. Using this voucher allows the user to receive a random reward from the prize pool
A section in the OKX Feed platform that shows community posts with negative factors impacting an asset. To access open the app Discover → Opportunities → Feed → Top Mentions

In general, Airdrop Vouchers are type promotional vouchers that can be distributed to users by crypto projects or exchanges either for free or in exchange for a small promotional service, and they can be used/redeemed against a couple of different services related to that project or exchange that distributed them.
Here on OKX, it's used to refer to a type of voucher that allows a user to create a bot with a certain investment amount for free. If the bot makes a profit, it goes to the user. If the bot closes with a loss, it is covered by OKX.

Health factor is a numerical indicator that reflects the safety of your deposited assets relative to your borrowed assets and their underlying value. A higher health factor indicates a safer position and lower risk of liquidation. If the health factor less than or equal to 1, your assets may be subject to liquidation.

Pre-market perpetuals is the product name of OKX's pre-market trading platform. It allows you to trade perpetual contracts on crypto that has not yet been officially listed on OKX. These futures, known as pre-market perpetual futures, track the price of these pre-listed crypto. They get converted to standard perpetual contracts once the crypto gets listed on OKX for spot trading. If the crypto doesn't get listed, pre-market perpetuals trading will end and the contracts will settle.

A sticker is a digital image or illustration that users can send in chats or posts to express emotions, reactions, or messages. Unlike emojis, stickers are often larger, more detailed, and can be animated.

A Crypto Asset Service Provider (CASP) is a legal or natural entity that provides services related to crypto assets, which include cryptocurrencies, tokens, and other digital assets. Referring to an asset no longer being denominated.

"Aped" means that someone — like a person you follow, a smart investor, or an influencer (in the case of scan mode) — acted on the same impulse and jumped into the same investment/portfolio as you. The outcome can go either way, but the key idea is that you weren’t alone in taking the leap.

The overall profit or loss percentage across all positions or trading activities, providing a comprehensive performance measure.
The combined fees and charges for both opening and closing positions, representing total transaction costs.
Trading orders specifically designed to close existing positions, such as market or limit orders for exits.
The quantity or volume of assets being closed in a single order when exiting a position.
Orders or requests submitted to close existing positions, which may be pending execution.
The last traded price of an asset at the end of a trading session or when a position is terminated.
The final execution price at which a position is closed on the exchange.
The total fees and charges incurred when closing a trading position, including transaction fees and spreads.
The profit or loss percentage calculated when a position is closed, showing the final performance of the trade.
The bid-ask spread percentage at the time a position is opened, expressed as a rate.
The difference between the bid and ask prices when closing a position, affecting the final trade execution.
The difference between the bid and ask prices when opening a position, representing the immediate cost of entering a trade.
The bid-ask spread percentage at the time a position is closed, affecting the final execution price.
The specific criteria or requirements that must be met to open a new trading position, including minimum balance or risk parameters.
The specific criteria or requirements that must be met to close a trading position, such as price levels or time conditions.
The interest rate charged when borrowing assets for margin trading or short selling positions on OKX.

A suite of OKX financial products designed to help users generate returns on their cryptocurrency holdings, including Earn products for staking and lending assets to earn interest or rewards, and Loan services for borrowing and lending to access liquidity or earn returns on deposits.

To launch/kick off an action. For OKEx and crypto trading, may refer to both "to trigger order" and "to trigger price".
to exchange one's onchain asset with a liquidity pool for another asset on the same blockchain.
to send free coins or tokens to wallet addresses to promote awareness of a crypto project within a crypto airdrop event.

As a user/trader, to "move position" means to move your current positions among your sub-accounts by using the "Move position" feature that is supported through API trading.
To audit means to perform a security review of a token’s properties to check if there are any contract exploits, hidden functions, or trading risks.

To perform an analysis and verification of a company's financial statements and records. Important Note: Please do not use "audit" together when referring to Proof of Reserves (PoR) we use alternatives such as: report, verification method, data capture. This is mandated by the GTM team.
Hedge: To use a Hedge strategy aiming to reducing the risk of investment by counterbalancing the potential losses of one investment with the gains of another.
The act of getting ownership of assets obtained through the OKX or Okcoin platform. For example earnings, rewards, or other promotions would all be claimed by someone.
to close a current position at market price and open a position of the same amount in the reserve direction.
to raise a dispute to try to solve the issue in such a situation: when a P2P order cannot proceed, one of the parties has the right.
to refuel using the "Refuel" Feature in DEX by using main network tokens as network fee.

A liquidation clearance fee is a fee imposed when a trader’s position is forcibly closed, or liquidated. This usually happens when the market moves against the position significantly in a very short term and the trader’s account lacks enough margin to maintain the position.
Avoid using liquidation fee, since the term “fee” may suggest a minor charge and imply that OKX profits from this situation, which is not the case. The goal is always to encourage prudent trading and risk management.
Campaign term. A voucher that is rewarded to campaign participants. Using this voucher allows the user to receive a random reward from the prize pool
A section in the OKX Feed platform that shows community posts with negative factors impacting an asset. To access open the app Discover → Opportunities → Feed → Top Mentions

In general, Airdrop Vouchers are type promotional vouchers that can be distributed to users by crypto projects or exchanges either for free or in exchange for a small promotional service, and they can be used/redeemed against a couple of different services related to that project or exchange that distributed them.
Here on OKX, it's used to refer to a type of voucher that allows a user to create a bot with a certain investment amount for free. If the bot makes a profit, it goes to the user. If the bot closes with a loss, it is covered by OKX.

Health factor is a numerical indicator that reflects the safety of your deposited assets relative to your borrowed assets and their underlying value. A higher health factor indicates a safer position and lower risk of liquidation. If the health factor less than or equal to 1, your assets may be subject to liquidation.

The full Chinese naming for Orbiter+ is 星球领航员, with 领航员 as the short form. Chinese content designers have the flexibility to use either the full or short form depending on context. In English contexts, using "Orbiter+" alone is sufficient.

Real-world assets (RWAs) are physical or traditional financial assets that exist outside the blockchain.

Face value is a financial term used to describe the nominal or dollar value of a security, as stated by its issuer.

Factory contract is a smart contract that programmatically deploys other contracts from a single template. It creates multiple identical or parameterized instances on demand, ensuring consistent logic and reduced deployment overhead. In our context, the factory creates the airdrop reward contracts for OKX Boost.

An insurance sector that covers against risks associated with DeFi investments, smart contract failures, wallet issues, and dApp/DAO hacks.

In another meaning (”blockchain insurance”) can be understood as traditional insurance that utilizes blockchain technology for more efficient and effective insurance services.

Onchain Indicator: Refers to metrics or data points that are captured and stored on a blockchain network. These indicators provide information about various aspects of the network, such as the amount of tokens in circulation, the volume of transactions, and other key metrics. They can be used to gain insight into the health and activity of a blockchain network and to inform investment decisions.
Cryptocurrency transactions that occur on the blockchain and remain dependent on the state of the blockchain for their validity

Describes technology or processes that enable assets or data to move between different blockchain networks.

A sequencer is a critical component within Layer 2 networks. Its main function is to receive and execute transactions from Layer 2 users. It then orders and compresses these transactions into a batch, which is finally submitted to Layer 1.
A certain percentage of copy traders' profits will be paid to the lead trader to reward their services. This process is called profit-sharing.

 During the redemption period, the assets are being processed for release and cannot be used or traded until they are fully available again.

An OKX account mode designed for advanced traders. In futures mode, users can trade spot, margin and derivatives. Traders can then choose multi-currency or portfolio mode depending on whether they want higher capital efficiency or easier hedging against risks.

Pre-market perpetuals is the product name of OKX's pre-market trading platform. It allows you to trade perpetual contracts on crypto that has not yet been officially listed on OKX. These futures, known as pre-market perpetual futures, track the price of these pre-listed crypto. They get converted to standard perpetual contracts once the crypto gets listed on OKX for spot trading. If the crypto doesn't get listed, pre-market perpetuals trading will end and the contracts will settle.

A sticker is a digital image or illustration that users can send in chats or posts to express emotions, reactions, or messages. Unlike emojis, stickers are often larger, more detailed, and can be animated.

A Crypto Asset Service Provider (CASP) is a legal or natural entity that provides services related to crypto assets, which include cryptocurrencies, tokens, and other digital assets. Referring to an asset no longer being denominated.

A threshold price set to warn traders before they place an order that might be risky.

A validator is a participant in a blockchain network responsible for verifying transactions and maintaining the integrity and security of the blockchain.

1. “Global” will be recognized by Brazilians and easily understood, while “Trading” clearly differentiates it from anything associated with Pay or Card and keeps it tailored to our target user.

"Aped" means that someone — like a person you follow, a smart investor, or an influencer (in the case of scan mode) — acted on the same impulse and jumped into the same investment/portfolio as you. The outcome can go either way, but the key idea is that you weren’t alone in taking the leap.

The overall profit or loss percentage across all positions or trading activities, providing a comprehensive performance measure.
The combined fees and charges for both opening and closing positions, representing total transaction costs.
Trading orders specifically designed to close existing positions, such as market or limit orders for exits.
The quantity or volume of assets being closed in a single order when exiting a position.
Orders or requests submitted to close existing positions, which may be pending execution.
The last traded price of an asset at the end of a trading session or when a position is terminated.
The final execution price at which a position is closed on the exchange.
The total fees and charges incurred when closing a trading position, including transaction fees and spreads.
The profit or loss percentage calculated when a position is closed, showing the final performance of the trade.
The bid-ask spread percentage at the time a position is opened, expressed as a rate.
The difference between the bid and ask prices when closing a position, affecting the final trade execution.
The difference between the bid and ask prices when opening a position, representing the immediate cost of entering a trade.
The bid-ask spread percentage at the time a position is closed, affecting the final execution price.
The specific criteria or requirements that must be met to open a new trading position, including minimum balance or risk parameters.
The specific criteria or requirements that must be met to close a trading position, such as price levels or time conditions.
The interest rate charged when borrowing assets for margin trading or short selling positions on OKX.

To launch/kick off an action. For OKEx and crypto trading, may refer to both "to trigger order" and "to trigger price".
to exchange one's onchain asset with a liquidity pool for another asset on the same blockchain.
to send free coins or tokens to wallet addresses to promote awareness of a crypto project within a crypto airdrop event.

As a user/trader, to "move position" means to move your current positions among your sub-accounts by using the "Move position" feature that is supported through API trading.
To audit means to perform a security review of a token’s properties to check if there are any contract exploits, hidden functions, or trading risks.

To perform an analysis and verification of a company's financial statements and records. Important Note: Please do not use "audit" together when referring to Proof of Reserves (PoR) we use alternatives such as: report, verification method, data capture. This is mandated by the GTM team.
Hedge: To use a Hedge strategy aiming to reducing the risk of investment by counterbalancing the potential losses of one investment with the gains of another.
The act of getting ownership of assets obtained through the OKX or Okcoin platform. For example earnings, rewards, or other promotions would all be claimed by someone.
to close a current position at market price and open a position of the same amount in the reserve direction.
to raise a dispute to try to solve the issue in such a situation: when a P2P order cannot proceed, one of the parties has the right.
to refuel using the "Refuel" Feature in DEX by using main network tokens as network fee.

A liquidation clearance fee is a fee imposed when a trader’s position is forcibly closed, or liquidated. This usually happens when the market moves against the position significantly in a very short term and the trader’s account lacks enough margin to maintain the position.
Avoid using liquidation fee, since the term “fee” may suggest a minor charge and imply that OKX profits from this situation, which is not the case. The goal is always to encourage prudent trading and risk management.
Campaign term. A voucher that is rewarded to campaign participants. Using this voucher allows the user to receive a random reward from the prize pool
A section in the OKX Feed platform that shows community posts with negative factors impacting an asset. To access open the app Discover → Opportunities → Feed → Top Mentions

In general, Airdrop Vouchers are type promotional vouchers that can be distributed to users by crypto projects or exchanges either for free or in exchange for a small promotional service, and they can be used/redeemed against a couple of different services related to that project or exchange that distributed them.
Here on OKX, it's used to refer to a type of voucher that allows a user to create a bot with a certain investment amount for free. If the bot makes a profit, it goes to the user. If the bot closes with a loss, it is covered by OKX.

Health factor is a numerical indicator that reflects the safety of your deposited assets relative to your borrowed assets and their underlying value. A higher health factor indicates a safer position and lower risk of liquidation. If the health factor less than or equal to 1, your assets may be subject to liquidation.

Pay is a service that streamlines crypto payments on our platform. For Chinese content, consistently use “Pay” across all products. In external communications like marketing, use the full brand name OKX Pay.

Centralized Exchange (CEX): A cryptocurrency exchange platform operated by a centralized entity that retains custody of crypto assets on a user's behalf. Centralized exchange refers to a digital platform that facilitates the trading, buying, and selling of various cryptocurrencies.

They have the expertise, credibility, and a huge follower base in their niche, which they can leverage to endorse your crypto products, services, or ideas, which makes them a brilliant source of organic promotion, marketing, and community building in the crypto space.
Impact value: The notional value used to determine the impact bid and ask price. Binance and Bybit call it "Impact margin notional". Ex.: Impact value ({ccy}), where {ccy} can be USDT, USDC, or USD.

Pre-quote is a type of pre-listing phase, where traders can place limit orders before a crypto is officially listed in spot trading. It's called "Pre-quote" because the final price of the asset hasn't been defined. The listing price will be defined by the index price.

Measures the average reward compared to the average risk of a position.

Risk/reward ratio = Avg PnL of losing positions / Avg PnL of winning positions

The currency used as collateral to open and maintain a margin position. When trading on margin, the margin currency is held in your account as a security deposit against potential losses. The required margin amount is calculated based on your position size and the applicable margin rate.

The MA helps to level the price data over a specified period by creating a constantly updated average price.
A simple moving average (SMA) is a calculation that takes the arithmetic mean of a given set of prices over a specific number of days in the past.

Weight Moving Average (WMA) is a moving average that assigns more weight to recent prices and less to older ones. Compared to a Simple Moving Average (MA), WMA responds faster to price changes, making it especially useful for identifying short-term trend shifts.

A liquidation clearance fee is a fee imposed when a trader’s position is forcibly closed, or liquidated. This usually happens when the market moves against the position significantly in a very short term and the trader’s account lacks enough margin to maintain the position.
Avoid using liquidation fee, since the term “fee” may suggest a minor charge and imply that OKX profits from this situation, which is not the case. The goal is always to encourage prudent trading and risk management.

Face value is a financial term used to describe the nominal or dollar value of a security, as stated by its issuer.

Factory contract is a smart contract that programmatically deploys other contracts from a single template. It creates multiple identical or parameterized instances on demand, ensuring consistent logic and reduced deployment overhead. In our context, the factory creates the airdrop reward contracts for OKX Boost.
Token/project name.

An insurance sector that covers against risks associated with DeFi investments, smart contract failures, wallet issues, and dApp/DAO hacks.

In another meaning (”blockchain insurance”) can be understood as traditional insurance that utilizes blockchain technology for more efficient and effective insurance services.

Onchain Indicator: Refers to metrics or data points that are captured and stored on a blockchain network. These indicators provide information about various aspects of the network, such as the amount of tokens in circulation, the volume of transactions, and other key metrics. They can be used to gain insight into the health and activity of a blockchain network and to inform investment decisions.
Cryptocurrency transactions that occur on the blockchain and remain dependent on the state of the blockchain for their validity
Onchain Earn is a collection of Earn products that used to be called DeFI and ETH2.0.
Describes technology or processes that enable assets or data to move between different blockchain networks.

A sequencer is a critical component within Layer 2 networks. Its main function is to receive and execute transactions from Layer 2 users. It then orders and compresses these transactions into a batch, which is finally submitted to Layer 1.
A certain percentage of copy traders' profits will be paid to the lead trader to reward their services. This process is called profit-sharing.
Flash Earn is an innovative earn product that lets you subscribe with USDT or other major crypto. Earn base crypto yields and get bonus airdrops of newly listed assets.
A beginner-friendly trading feature on OKX that lets users quickly purchase or sell crypto using fiat currencies or payment methods such as bank transfer, card, or third-party providers.

A feature that lets you sell crypto instantly at a quoted price. The price is locked for a short time while you confirm, protecting you from sudden market changes. The security fund, previously known as the insurance fund, is used to offset potential losses from forced liquidations. When a trader's position is liquidated, any excess assets are added to the security fund. If the security fund is depleted, then the exchange must trigger ADL.

The distribution of assets, rewards, or tokens to users based on specific criteria such as staking amount, subscription size, or participation level.

Information that identifies where a user’s deposited money or crypto assets originate from.

 During the redemption period, the assets are being processed for release and cannot be used or traded until they are fully available again.

Owner (Crypto)

An OKX account mode designed for advanced traders. In futures mode, users can trade spot, margin and derivatives. Traders can then choose multi-currency or portfolio mode depending on whether they want higher capital efficiency or easier hedging against risks.

An advanced OKX account mode designed for professional traders. In multi-currency margin mode, users can trade spot, margin and derivatives. Crypto assets are valued in USD when calculating margin. Cross positions with same or different margin crypto share the risk, which can reduce the overall liquidation risk.
Name of a Web3 product. Boost allows users to join different events to earn token rewards.

A basic OKX account mode designed for spot and futures traders. In futures mode, users can trade spot, margin and derivatives. Trading symbols with the same margin crypto share the margin, and cross positions using the same margin crypto share the risk.

Dust means low-value non-native tokens. Dust converter is a feature that allows an one-time swap of multiple such tokens (dust) to native tokens.

Pre-market perpetuals is the product name of OKX's pre-market trading platform. It allows you to trade perpetual contracts on crypto that has not yet been officially listed on OKX. These futures, known as pre-market perpetual futures, track the price of these pre-listed crypto. They get converted to standard perpetual contracts once the crypto gets listed on OKX for spot trading. If the crypto doesn't get listed, pre-market perpetuals trading will end and the contracts will settle.
A unique invite link is a special, one-of-a-kind URL that gives the user  access to a group, a feature etc.
This role focuses on identifying and generating new business opportunities rather than closing deals.
An emergency escape usually refers to a mechanism or action that allows users to quickly withdraw, secure, or move their crypto assets in the event of a security threat or system failure.

A sticker is a digital image or illustration that users can send in chats or posts to express emotions, reactions, or messages. Unlike emojis, stickers are often larger, more detailed, and can be animated.

A Crypto Asset Service Provider (CASP) is a legal or natural entity that provides services related to crypto assets, which include cryptocurrencies, tokens, and other digital assets. The term is widely used in regulatory contexts, especially in jurisdictions like the European Union under the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation.
Referring to an asset no longer being denominated.
Referring to denomination: An asset being listed.
A discount rate is the rate at which you discount future cash flows or utility value back to its present value.
AUM stands for “Assets Under Management”.
Expiries mark the deadline by which the holder must exercise their rights.
A user that takes out a loan from another user under an agreement to pay it back later, typically with interest.
A lender is a user that extends credit to other entities, with the expectation that the full amount of the loan will be repaid.
A threshold price set to warn traders before they place an order that might be risky.
Price level is the average of current prices of cryptocurrencies and assets.
A "scale order" is a trading strategy where a large order is broken down into smaller, sequential orders placed at different price levels.
A validator is a participant in a blockchain network responsible for verifying transactions and maintaining the integrity and security of the blockchain.

Terms of use, also known as terms and conditions, are a set of rules and guidelines that govern how a website, app, or service can be used. They outline what users can and cannot do, and what rights the service provider maintains, essentially acting as a contract between the provider and the user
The action of lending an asset.

As we now offer crypto derivatives in Brazil, we’re launching a new type of account that offers access to derivatives via an offshore entity. Following Brazilian colleagues’ suggestion, we now name it as “Global Trading account” with “Global Trading” non-translatable and capitalized for 2 reasons:
1. “Global” will be recognized by Brazilians and easily understood, while “Trading” clearly differentiates it from anything associated with Pay or Card and keeps it tailored to our target user.
2. Keeping the name always in English also helps us strengthen our requirement that this be clearly a separate experience and product from OKX Brazil.

The umbrella phrase we use to describe any laws and regulations that might affect the KYC process. Instead of naming specific laws, we should only use "local laws" for simplicity. This phrase replaced "Travel Rule".

"Aped" means that someone — like a person you follow, a smart investor, or an influencer (in the case of scan mode) — acted on the same impulse and jumped into the same investment/portfolio as you. The outcome can go either way, but the key idea is that you weren’t alone in taking the leap.

Aped in, Aped together, Aped with me => these terms are variations for the CN phrase "同车", depending on the context.
The overall profit or loss percentage across all positions or trading activities, providing a comprehensive performance measure.
The combined fees and charges for both opening and closing positions, representing total transaction costs.
Trading orders specifically designed to close existing positions, such as market or limit orders for exits.
The quantity or volume of assets being closed in a single order when exiting a position.
Orders or requests submitted to close existing positions, which may be pending execution.
The last traded price of an asset at the end of a trading session or when a position is terminated.
The final execution price at which a position is closed on the exchange.
The total fees and charges incurred when closing a trading position, including transaction fees and spreads.
The profit or loss percentage calculated when a position is closed, showing the final performance of the trade.
The bid-ask spread percentage at the time a position is opened, expressed as a rate.
The difference between the bid and ask prices when closing a position, affecting the final trade execution.
The difference between the bid and ask prices when opening a position, representing the immediate cost of entering a trade.
The bid-ask spread percentage at the time a position is closed, affecting the final execution price.
The specific criteria or requirements that must be met to open a new trading position, including minimum balance or risk parameters.
The specific criteria or requirements that must be met to close a trading position, such as price levels or time conditions.
The interest rate charged when borrowing assets for margin trading or short selling positions on OKX.

A suite of OKX financial products designed to help users generate returns on their cryptocurrency holdings, including Earn products for staking and lending assets to earn interest or rewards, and Loan services for borrowing and lending to access liquidity or earn returns on deposits.

A "rate-up coupon" is a promotional tool, often used in the context of a trading bot campaign (usually the smart arbitrage bot), that offers a boost to the interest rate or yield on an investment. Essentially, it provides an additional, temporary, or exclusive benefit to a trader beyond the standard APR.
To launch/kick off an action. For OKEx and crypto trading, may refer to both "to trigger order" and "to trigger price".
to exchange one's onchain asset with a liquidity pool for another asset on the same blockchain.
to send free coins or tokens to wallet addresses to promote awareness of a crypto project within a crypto airdrop event.
OKX Web3 Security is an umbrella term for a set of pages and features under the OKX Web3 Security team, including malicious app/DApp/address detection, phising site identification etc.
As a user/trader, to "move position" means to move your current positions among your sub-accounts by using the "Move position" feature that is supported through API trading.
To audit means to perform a security review of a token’s properties to check if there are any contract exploits, hidden functions, or trading risks.

To perform an analysis and verification of a company's financial statements and records. Important Note: Please do not use "audit" together when referring to Proof of Reserves (PoR) we use alternatives such as: report, verification method, data capture. This is mandated by the GTM team.
Hedge: To use a Hedge strategy aiming to reducing the risk of investment by counterbalancing the potential losses of one investment with the gains of another.
The act of getting ownership of assets obtained through the OKX or Okcoin platform. For example earnings, rewards, or other promotions would all be claimed by someone.
to close a current position at market price and open a position of the same amount in the reserve direction.
to raise a dispute to try to solve the issue in such a situation: when a P2P order cannot proceed, one of the parties has the right.
to refuel using the "Refuel" Feature in DEX by using main network tokens as network fee.

A liquidation clearance fee is a fee imposed when a trader’s position is forcibly closed, or liquidated. This usually happens when the market moves against the position significantly in a very short term and the trader’s account lacks enough margin to maintain the position.
Avoid using liquidation fee, since the term “fee” may suggest a minor charge and imply that OKX profits from this situation, which is not the case. The goal is always to encourage prudent trading and risk management.
Campaign term. A voucher that is rewarded to campaign participants. Using this voucher allows the user to receive a random reward from the prize pool
A section in the OKX Feed platform that shows community posts with negative factors impacting an asset. To access open the app Discover → Opportunities → Feed → Top Mentions

In general, Airdrop Vouchers are type promotional vouchers that can be distributed to users by crypto projects or exchanges either for free or in exchange for a small promotional service, and they can be used/redeemed against a couple of different services related to that project or exchange that distributed them.
Here on OKX, it's used to refer to a type of voucher that allows a user to create a bot with a certain investment amount for free. If the bot makes a profit, it goes to the user. If the bot closes with a loss, it is covered by OKX.

Health factor is a numerical indicator that reflects the safety of your deposited assets relative to your borrowed assets and their underlying value. A higher health factor indicates a safer position and lower risk of liquidation. If the health factor less than or equal to 1, your assets may be subject to liquidation.

Pay is a service that streamlines crypto payments on our platform. For Chinese content, consistently use “Pay” across all products. In external communications like marketing, use the full brand name OKX Pay.

Centralized Exchange (CEX): A cryptocurrency exchange platform operated by a centralized entity that retains custody of crypto assets on a user's behalf. Centralized exchange refers to a digital platform that facilitates the trading, buying, and selling of various cryptocurrencies.

In general, KOLs are experts with a strong reputation in a specific field. 

In crypto industry, Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs) can play a crucial role in influencing and shaping public opinions around your crypto project, product, or service.

They have the expertise, credibility, and a huge follower base in their niche, which they can leverage to endorse your crypto products, services, or ideas, which makes them a brilliant source of organic promotion, marketing, and community building in the crypto space.
Impact value: The notional value used to determine the impact bid and ask price. Binance and Bybit call it "Impact margin notional". Ex.: Impact value ({ccy}), where {ccy} can be USDT, USDC, or USD.
BTC Select is a collection of BTC-USDT Dual Investment products designed to help users make use of their idle BTC.

Pre-quote is a type of pre-listing phase, where traders can place limit orders before a crypto is officially listed in spot trading. It's called "Pre-quote" because the final price of the asset hasn't been defined. The listing price will be defined by the index price.

Measures the average reward compared to the average risk of a position.

Risk/reward ratio = Avg PnL of losing positions / Avg PnL of winning positions

Trader mode is a new feature for Web3 wallet and DEX that transforms a regular EOA account into a TEE (Trusted Execution Environment)-based account. Trader mode gives users access to more advanced trading modes and features.
An OTC version of our Structured Products - only available to VIPs through their VIP relationship managers.

An alert is a system-generated notification that draws the user’s attention to important information, status changes, risks, or required actions. Alerts are typically time-sensitive and appear in-app, as a banner, modal, push notification, or message.

The currency used as collateral to open and maintain a margin position. When trading on margin, the margin currency is held in your account as a security deposit against potential losses. The required margin amount is calculated based on your position size and the applicable margin rate.

The MA helps to level the price data over a specified period by creating a constantly updated average price.
A simple moving average (SMA) is a calculation that takes the arithmetic mean of a given set of prices over a specific number of days in the past.

Weight Moving Average (WMA) is a moving average that assigns more weight to recent prices and less to older ones. Compared to a Simple Moving Average (MA), WMA responds faster to price changes, making it especially useful for identifying short-term trend shifts.
